text: Pre-meiotic male germ cells are outside of the blood-testis barrier and thus are exposed if there is systemic distribution. Exposure of pre-meiotic male germ cells to DNA alkylating agents results in DNA alkyl adducts. Replication of DNA with alkyl adducts thus can cause mutations in these cells. Fertilization of an egg by sperm containing mutations causes an increase in the number of mutations that are transmitted to their offspring.
